content.full_text:
Fern Por-rrrcAl Pnecrrcss CoN,rMrssroN
-128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacrarnento. CA 9-581-l-1329
(916) -312-5660 r Fax t9l6) _322-0886
September 16,2009
Ms. Stephanie Loose, Treasurer
Citizens for Carmel Valley
412 South Main St.
Salinas, CA 93901-3306
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-214
Dear Ms. Loose:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act").1
QUESTION
"Citizens for Carmel Valley" is a political committee supporting the Carmel
Valley incorporation Measure G and new Town Council candidiies, five of whom are on
the board of the Committee. Is the Committee properly characterized as a candidate
committee, a primarily formed committee, or a general purpose committee, and how
should the Committee properly disclose the money which ii spends supporting the
measure and supporting the candidates?
CONCLI]SION
Because five of the new Town Council candidates are on the board of the
committee directing its operations, the committee may be properly characterized as a
joint candidate committee. The committee's disclosure of money spent supporting the
incorporation Measure G and supporting the candidates is discurr"d i, the analysis
below.
' The Political Reform r\ct is contained in Government Code Sestions g1000 through 91014. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 through I 8997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-09-214
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are treasurer of "citizens_for carmel valley" (Fppc ID No. l3lgl00), the
political committee set up to support Proposition G and affiliated candidates on the
November 2009 Monterey County ballot. You are requesting advice from the
commission regarding the filing requirements for this Committee.
The primary focus of the Committee is to support the passage of Measure G,
which would create Carmel Valley as its own incorporated town irr"t tont"i"y counry.
Along with Measure G, the ballot will contain a lisiof candidates running fi town
Council, should Measure G pass. The Committee will therefore also focus on the
election of five of these Town Council candidates. Although the Committee is not
certain exactly what percentage of its activities and expenditures will relate to the
measure as compared to the candidates, it anticipates spending approximately 75 percent
of its funds on the measure and 25 percent of its funds o, tt " "aniidates, although this
allocation may change one way or the otheras the campaign proceeds. For example, the
committee could put-out a fgur-page mail piece, with tirree pag", discussing Measure G
and one page devoted to the slate of five Town Council candidates. More information
about the committee's activities to date can be found on its website:
http ://citizensforcarnrel valley. com/
The five candidates whom the Committee will support all sit on the ,.steering
committee" for the. committee, participating in decisions about how the committee raises
and spends its funds. Because these five candidates for Town Council t uu" ..u rig-;ffi;
influence on the actions or decisions of the Committee," they will be deemed to ..control,,
the Committee' as that term is defined in Government Code Section g2016. These
candidates do not anticipate raising and spending any money to promote their candidacies
other than the activities of the committee, and thereiore do not anticipate opening up
their own campaign committees or bank accounts.
You request guidance from the Commission about whether the Committee is
properly characteriz* ur_u "primarily formed," ,.general puqpose,, or..candidate,,
committee and how the committee should prop".ly disclose the money which it spends
supporting the measure and supporting the iandidates.
ANALYSIS
Because five of the new Town Council candidates are on the board of the
co.mmittee directing its operations, the committee may be properly characterized as a
joint candidate election committee.2
. 
2 gection 82016(a) states, among other things, that a candidate controls a committee ..if he or she
has a significant influence on the actibns or decisions of the committee.,,


File No. A-09-214
Page No. 3
The Act does not prohibit joint candidate election committees. (See Miranda
Advice Letter, No. 4-08-033, and Bott Advice Letter, No. A-91-3g6.) We have advised,
however, that a committee cannot be jointly controlled by candidates being voted on in a
jurisdiction in which the candidates seeking public office are subject to liriits on
campaign contributions. (See generally, DormanAdvice Letter, No. I-92-4g3.) you
have informed us that there are no local campaign contribution limits applicable in this
election. Thus, the Act permits a joint candidate committee controlled ty fiu. of the new
Town Council candidates.
Please note, however, that Section 85201 requires that all contributions or loans
made to a candidate or to the candidate's controlledtommittee shall be deposited in a
single campaign bank account established for the specific election. The CLmmission has
interpreted this to mean that a candidate for elective office may have orfy o"" 
"umpaign
bank account and controlled committee for each election. (Regulation rsszr.) Thus, if
the new Town Council candidates form a joint committe" rtr tf," up.o-i"j"iectlon, all
contributions and loans made to the candidates for that election must be delposited in the
joint campaign bank account, and they may not have another controlled committee for
that election.
The name of the Committee would have to include the last names of the five
Town council candidates, the office sought and year. (Regulation tg+02, cijy
enclosed.) On the Form 460,Part5, you will provide the riame of the candidates
controlling the committee and indicate the office sought. Because more than one
candidate is controlling the committee, you may copy Part 5 as many times as needed to
include the required information for all controlling candidates. (Campaign Manu al Z p.
6-3,5t2007.)
with respect to verification, the Committee treasurer or assistant treasurer must
review anfl sign the campaign statement- Regarding verification by the controlling
candidates, Regulation 18430, copy enclosed proviies as follows:
"(b) If a committee other than a legislative caucus committee is
controlled by more than one candidate, all campaign statements filed by
the committee shall be signed and verified by:
(l) All the conrrolling candidates in the case of a committee
controlled by two or three candidates;
(2) one of the controlling candidates, on behalf of all the controlling
candidates, in the case of a committee controlled by more than three
candidates."3
As a candidate election committee, the Committee would disclose the portion of
any campaign advertisements attributable to promoting the Town Council "oniidut", o,
I Also see Campaign Manual 2 p.6-3,5/ZC/|)7


File No. A-09-214
Page No.4
expenditures on Schedule E of its Form 460 campaign statements (with the applicable
expenditure code). The Committee would disclose the portion attributable to promoting
the ballot measure as an "independent expenditure" on Schedules D and E. Bicause any
money the Committee spends supporting the ballot measure would be considered an
independent expenditure for the ballot measure, the Committee would also have to file
Supplemental lndependent Expenditure Reports (Form 465) in connection with the
Measure G election. The Committee would also have to file lndependent Expenditure
Reports (Form 496), for independent expenditures made during the 16 days bifore the
election.
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
W, p, tJo
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
HPWjgl
Enclosures
